The '''plasma battle droid''' was a gargantuan, 12-[[Foot/Legends|foot]] version of the standard [[B1-series battle droid/Legends|B1-series battle droid]] used by the [[Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation]]. They fired lightning-like [[Plasma/Legends|plasma]] blasts that were powerful enough to overwhelm even a [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]]. They were designed to disrupt enemy infrastructure, and as a result were poorly-defended.<ref name="JPB guide" />

These rare units were distinguished from their B1 [[Battle droid/Legends|battle droid]] cousins by the green coloration of the [[Droid/Legends|droid's]] shoulder plates and enormous height. Plasma battle droids' blasts and appearance made them both formidable and deadly, however they were extremely inaccurate as well as clumsy.

In [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]],<ref name="The New Essential Chronology">The [[Star Wars video games|video game]] itself refers to its events taking place during the [[Invasion of Naboo/Legends|Invasion of Naboo]]. ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' establishes that the invasion happens in [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]].</ref> they were used to guard the [[Theed Hangar/Legends|Theed Hangar]] [[Great Hall (Naboo)/Legends|Great Hall]] for a short time during the [[Invasion of Naboo/Legends|Invasion of Naboo]]. Neither droid survived when attacked by [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] warriors, soon after the [[Capture of Theed/Legends|Fall of Theed]]. Several more were found in the swamps near the [[Gungan Sacred Place/Legends|Gungan Ruins]], but they [[Battle of the Sacred Place|suffered]] the same fate as their predecessors.<ref name="SW:JPB">[[Star Wars: Episode I Jedi Power Battles|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''Jedi Power Battles'']]</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
Plasma battle droids appear in the [[2000]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Episode I Jedi Power Battles]]''. Their appearance in a mission contradicts [[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''The Phantom Menace'']]. In the game, the Jedi face two plasma battle droids, whereas in the film they face a contingent of battle droids.
